edge_cases_considered: TUI local turn bypasses app-core Agent StreamingGuard
Sidecar direct OpenAI/Claude HTTP paths bypass Provider::complete
Doctor/examples/tests can drive concrete providers outside Agent loops
MCP native tool callbacks can occur while a provider stream is active
MCP post-stream tool calls occur between provider requests
Direct debug/manual/mcp-serve MCP tool calls occur outside any provider turn
Batch can run MCP subcalls concurrently
Startup recovery spawns continuations and should not hold a startup lease for their full provider turns
TUI MCP init currently blocks on connect_all, unlike app-core background registration
evidence: crates/jcode-app-core/src/agent/turn_loops.rs:11-22 creates StreamingGuard and cancel guard for blocking Agent turn
crates/jcode-app-core/src/agent/turn_loops.rs:573-608 handles native tool callbacks during provider stream
crates/jcode-app-core/src/agent/turn_streaming_mpsc.rs:97-112 creates StreamingGuard and cancel guard for MPSC turn
crates/jcode-app-core/src/agent/turn_streaming_mpsc.rs:870-899 handles native tool callbacks during provider stream
crates/jcode-app-core/src/agent/turn_streaming_mpsc.rs:1399-1440 spawns registry.execute for post-stream local tools
crates/jcode-base/src/sidecar.rs:651-681 and 824-929 are direct sidecar HTTP backends that bypass Provider::complete
crates/jcode-app-core/src/tool/mod.rs:890-927 backgrounds MCP connect_all and live tool registration
crates/jcode-app-core/src/tool/mcp.rs:88-143 MCP management execute dispatches lifecycle actions
src/cli/mcp_serve.rs:163-210 inbound tools/call maps to daemon debug tool command
crates/jcode-app-core/src/server.rs:787-800 startup recovery restores session registry and MCP tools
crates/jcode-app-core/src/server.rs:1126-1128 calls startup recovery after accept loops are live
artifact.findings: Read-only analysis completed and written to /tmp/F01-R-entry-families-report.md. Provider-turn families found: (1) trait/default boundary in jcode-provider-core, (2) TUI local interactive complete_split loop, (3) app-core blocking Agent run_turn, (4) app-core MPSC/server/remote run_turn_streaming_mpsc, (5) MultiProvider/failover dispatch plus concrete runtimes, (6) sidecar/direct utilities and probes. MCP execution families found: (1) registration/startup advertisement, (2) generic Registry::execute and debug/manual dispatch, (3) MCP management tool, (4) MCP proxy tools and McpManager call/connect-on-first-call, (5) shared/owned MCP client/pool JSON-RPC execution, (6) external `jcode mcp-serve` stdio seam. True common provider-turn guard boundary is not Agent::run_turn or MCP. It is the Provider `complete`/`complete_split` request contract, but the guard must be held by a returned EventStream wrapper so it lasts until stream EOF/error/cancel/drop. MCP executions are not provider request opens, so MCP files are not the right central seam unless the desired invariant is a broader agent-turn/resource lease. Startup recovery needs a separate bounded `StartupRecoveryLease` acquired in recover_headless_sessions_on_startup after non-empty candidate detection and released after enumeration/scheduling/timing, with global/per-session TTLs. Spawned continuation provider turns should use the provider-turn guard, not inherit the startup lease.
open_questions: Whether direct sidecar HTTP requests should receive the same guard as provider streams or a separate sidecar request guard depends on product intent.
Whether standalone examples/doctor/tests must be covered in production guard invariants or only app/runtime paths need coverage.
Exact TTL values for StartupRecoveryLease should be chosen by product expectations and existing reload SLAs.
artifact.validation: Ran read-only rg/python/read searches only. Key commands: `rg -n --glob '*.rs' --glob '!target/**' '\\.(complete|complete_split)\\('`; `rg -n --glob '*.rs' --glob '!target/**' '(registry\\.execute|execute_tool\\(|call_tool\\(|register_mcp_tools|connect_all\\(\\)|McpClient::connect|tools/call|run_mcp_serve_command)'`; Python scan for Provider impl lines. Wrote report to /tmp/F01-R-entry-families-report.md. No repo edits, network calls, credentials, or destructive operations.
